event: Todd allegedly attempted to pump DGTX using a bot on third-party exchanges that was designed to buy more than it sold.
metric_signal: Persistent net buying by an interested party can support a token price even when the trading loses money.

event: Todd allegedly filled large over-the-counter DGTX purchase orders on third-party exchanges rather than out of the Digitex treasury.
metric_signal: Routing buyer demand through public markets can create visible price impact instead of an off-market treasury transfer.

event: Digitex Futures required users to deposit DGTX into accounts to margin futures trading, according to the CFTC.
metric_signal: Native-token utility can create a platform incentive to defend public market price.

event: The CFTC filed its complaint against Todd, Digitex LLC, Digitex Limited, Digitex Software Limited, and Blockster Holdings.
metric_signal: Civil enforcement identified attempted manipulation of DGTX and registration failures.

event: A federal court issued a default judgment granting a permanent injunction.
metric_signal: The court resolved the action and imposed trading and registration bans.

event: The CFTC announced more than $15 million in ordered payments, including $3,912,220 in disgorgement and $11,736,660 in civil monetary penalty.
metric_signal: Final sanctions quantify the cost of attempted native-token price manipulation and related violations.
